title: Progress
wikitext:
[]: Progress...             Quote Post by qu4d » Tue Oct 16, 2012 9:56 am A few days ago in the last sequel thread, I just stated one thought about NME being a sequel on a meta-level and Lurkis wrote: I feel like an idiot for not seeing that before, thank you for you insight :3

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Tue Oct 16, 2012 10:04 am A lot of people may tell you that this mirroring in Rebuild is inserted mostly to entertain fans who have seen the original series/movies, but I tend to see it the same way you do. I did notice these things, but I didn't interpret them the same way you did. Very good insight!

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Oct 16, 2012 10:35 am It can be seen in this meta way as a sequel to the characters in NGE, though within NME itself the characters of Rei and Shinji have clearly progressed from their flaws in You Are (Not) Alone to their present circumstances in You Can (Not) Advance . And I think strictly interpreting what Shinji and Rei do in You Can (Not) Advance as a meta progression from their faults in NGE takes away their progression from the progress they've made from their faults in Yo Are (Not) Alone . In this regard, it isn't so much evidence for or against a Sequel Theory, but rather which interpretation of the events in 2.22 are stronger and resonate with today's audience. And for 2.22 to call back a lot of moments from a show made 14 years prior to the film's release would take away that impact for much of today's viewers when trying to view those moments as a progression of the events in NGE. That being said, these moments to give older fans of the Evangelion franchise that sort of "meta appreciation" for the new movies. (Either that, or "meta hate," depending on how you felt about Rei's simple slap-deflecting ability.) Many older fans have always wanted to see Shinji finally take the reigns of Unit 01 in a very determined fashion in battle, and we finally got to see just that. Some of us always wanted to see Rei finally be more assertive with others much sooner than she did in the NGE series, and we got to see that. But we got to see it in a way that was consistent and contained within the see new films themselves, independent of the older series. That's not to say that they must be devoid of all of the emotions that the previous series brought about, but it was not dependent on it in order to make that leap of character development between the unassertiveness of the character in 1.11 to the extreme assertiveness of some of the characters in 2.22. In short, I guess it's like seeing the Adam West Batman, and then seeing Christopher Nolan's Batman films shortly thereafter. You can enjoy the more serious tone in the new films compared to the older TV series, just like you can enjoy the assertiveness in the new Eva films as opposed to the older TV series, but one is not dependent on the other in order to tell their own story as dynamically as it did. Adam West is still campy and Chris Nolan is still gritty, regardless of which one you've seen. Likewise, one can walk into NME without ever seeing the TV series and still enjoy the character development, but those who did see the TV series might enjoy the character development a bit more, maybe even equating it to being positively fanservice-y with how assertive Shinji is in 2.22 as compared to the older TV series. (Either that, or negatively fanservice-y, depending on how you felt about Shinji's simple war cry.)

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Tue Oct 16, 2012 11:22 am I like the whole Meta-Sequel idea, more than I like the sequel theory itself (and I used to like it a lot). It makes sense that Anno would do something like this, especially considering he does various other meta things as well. Really nice evidence too, qu4d. And while Q and Final are supposed to be totally off the rails as compared to 1.0 being very faithful and 2.0 being a little different but largely similar, I hope we see more of this meta-sequalness.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Oct 16, 2012 3:55 pm On a meta level, you could say that the place they all come from - Anno's head - improved. Then again, if the events went down just as they do in the original, that would be boring, hm? I do say that this is why you should watch the original first, because scenes like these are awesome on their own, but awesomer if you know a sadder version of them. You expect the hero to rescue his ladyfriend... except when you now that last time, she was blown to hell along with the mecha. So, people were actually, properly surprised when Rei got saved or did not let herself be slapped. But I don't think it's so much a concious theme as it is Anno just wanting to do some interesting new stuff and changes that had to me made due to insertig Mari and compressing stuff into movie format - the sleepover scene, for example, was added as a second thought according to CR - The authors thought Asuka's transistion from "hates teamwork" to "totally Tsundere about Shinji" needed more accentuating. I guess it ended up looking similar because the same people worked on it. On the other hand, the Elevator scene looks like the one from episode 22 and fills that role in Asuka's developement, but as for Rei's, it's more of an alagon to that one conversation in episode 16 (Rei actually talking back to Asuka in a Shinji-related manner) and the one she had with a very different doomed EVA 03 pilot in episode 17 ("You got a thing for Shinji, don't cha?") Otherwise, other parts/scenes that could be seen as "parallels" are a) One word: Pyramid stomping b) Gendo's office scene. Original Gendo: "I assume we won't meet again, Son, am am dissapoint." *this is the last proper conversation they ever have. Also, Gendo directly gave up* Rebuild Gendo: *actually makes one last attempt to act like a father and offers a piece of advice* c) Asuka. Just Asuka. She gets to become a better human being, sort out her conflicting priorities and bond with Misato. Misato actually thanks Asuka hereand gains some insight into her... in the original, she totally fell for Asuka's facade untill it was way too late.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Tue Oct 16, 2012 4:14 pm Kendrix wrote: On a meta level, you could say that the place they all come from - Anno's head - improved.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Tue Oct 16, 2012 4:47 pm Ah, but knowing her past doesn't mean knowing her as a person. It's weird to comment on Asuka's 'progress' anyway, though, because Shikinami is almost an entirely different character. She has no bearing on Soryu.

[]:             Quote Post by Brainman » Tue Oct 16, 2012 4:53 pm To me it seems more like a series of "what if's" than an actual attempt at fixing or making Rebuild a squeakuel. A spiritual squeakuel perhaps. But I don't know about it being a literal one. In the end, I guess I'm still just kind of puzzled by 2.0. I just don't think I "get it" yet. I mean, Shinji becoming a super sayain at the end? Well, OK, some people may have wanted that but to me it's a little off-putting. I suppose you could call me pessimistic, but most of the changes got a big "yeah right" from me as a response. That's why I'm mostly looking forward to 3.0. I need more data. Because from where I'm standing now, I can't synch with it at all, so to speak. Maybe it's because of my familiarity with the original, but I can't seem to reconcile the differences very well. I mean, is it Asuka or isn't it? Nobody ever gives a straight answer. In some arguments, she's the same person, in others she's a totally different entity. In the end I'm not really inspired by Asuka's arc in 2.0, but actually just confused and depressed by it. I'm hoping 3.0 helps me sort it out.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Tue Oct 16, 2012 7:14 pm Der Kommissar wrote: It's weird to comment on Asuka's 'progress' anyway, though, because Shikinami is almost an entirely different character. She has no bearing on Soryu.

[]:             Quote Post by Nonoriri » Tue Oct 16, 2012 10:05 pm Shinji in EoE "Why not try being nice to me :(" ---- acid trip (No.) Asuka tries to be nice ---> Almost dies. "Progress" :P

[]:             Quote Post by Lurkis » Tue Oct 16, 2012 10:34 pm When I said I felt like an idiot for not seeing it, I was referring specifically to the connection of the title 'you can (not) advance' followed by the advancement of characters in specific scenes. (Asuka laying back to back, Rei defending etc) When you lay it out like that it becomes the most obvious thing in the world XD and by far one of the best connections to be found iin NME Again, thank you for pointing it out :)

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Oct 17, 2012 12:13 am Brainman wrote: I mean, is it Asuka or isn't it? Nobody ever gives a straight answer. In some arguments, she's the same person, in others she's a totally different entity.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Oct 17, 2012 4:18 am Warren Peace wrote: Even NGE presents Asuka as "a totally different entity" by the end. The differences between Asuka (Episode 8) and Asuka (Episode 22) is WAY more vast than the difference between Shikinami and Soryu. I suspect Rebuild's point is the same: experience and nurture is just as important as nature.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Sat Dec 14, 2013 4:18 pm No idea why/how I missed that before, but after I heard it now again in the german 3.33 dub, it seemed so obvious 00:45:17 {Shinji} Well, how do I make music that sounds even better? 00:45:21 {Kaworu} Practice and more practice. 00:55:32 {Kaworu} Do the same thing over and over. 00:55:34 {Kaworu} Keep going until you're happy with it. 00:55:38 {Kaworu} That's the only way.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Dec 14, 2013 5:40 pm ^ That line was noticed very early after Q"s theatrical release, it's yet another of the weird stuff Kaworu says that could hints that he's a time looper or a dimensional wanderer. On a side-note, in your first post you wrote a list of situations that ended up better in Rebuild than in NGE, well that was before Q was released, but seeing how the last example ended(Shinji pseudo-evolving EVA-01), you should change "better outcome" by "different", although "stronger" pretty much correspond too with what happened with Shinji!

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 14, 2013 8:43 pm That's a philosophic question: Is it better to go along with the flow of things others have led you to, feeling like torching the planet, or not going through with it exactly when Gendo/Seele/ultimately Yui wanted him to and reach a positive or at least hopeful outcome (Impact reversed, toys needed for further impacts neutralized) Or to tell destiny to go screw itself and try to become an active player in the events, and utterly failing? Objectively, the planet being permanently torched is obviously worse, but that didn't depend on anything Shinji knew about/had access to. While I agree that these lines have a lot of overlooked significance (Main question of the movie: "Alright. We've agreed that you have to take risks to archieve things. But what if it 'splodes in my face after all? Which is particularly likely if I'm a clueless beginner at this." Answer: Common life lesson: "a) You don't have to be perfect b) stand up one more time than you fell down. Let's hope that, in time, the seed Kaworu planted there will germinate. ) was it really necessary to dig out this old thing?

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Sat Dec 21, 2013 11:02 pm I wonder if Unit 01's soul has been removed in NTE. It's interesting to note how much power plays are in each of the comparisons. Neither of the compared shots are small things that can just be brushed off. If you look at them, relative to the worlds they live in, they're actually pretty big. Shinji moving from sleeping alone, on the cold floor, compared to sleeping beside Asuka, albeit back-to-back, is actually a pretty bold change in power. Yet they're subtle enough to elude our eyes at first sight.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Dec 21, 2013 11:19 pm unitM wrote: I wonder if Unit 01's soul has been removed in NTE. It's interesting to note how much power plays are in each of the comparisons. Neither of the compared shots are small things that can just be brushed off. If you look at them, relative to the worlds they live in, they're actually pretty big. Shinji moving from sleeping alone, on the cold floor, compared to sleeping beside Asuka, albeit back-to-back, is actually a pretty bold change in power. Yet they're subtle enough to elude our eyes at first sight.

[]:             Quote Post by unitM » Sun Dec 22, 2013 3:45 pm Chuckman wrote: Maybe in ten years there will be a whole new continuity of UHD 4K movies and they'll actually smooch . As to Yui's soul, at first I thought that Yui is absent in NTE, for various reasons, but it's not consistent one way or another. The hand doesn't move to protect Shinji from the lights, but the lights don't fall, either, and while "Yui's refusal" of the dummy plug can be explained away, the Eva berserking while Shinji is unconcious during the Sachiel fight really can't. Having looked over these issues I think that the lights and the general lack of activity from Yui following the bardiel battle are more metatextual than some kind of clue about the secrets of the inner workings of the Eva (NTE cares even less for technobabble and how things "work" than NGE does; things happen because they need to happen). The point that's being made is that NGE was not really Shinji's story. He's a passive actor and things go on around him or happen to him and he has very little agency or effect on the narrative or his own growth. NTE rejects that. He must master himself. No one will save him from a falling light fixture. He's on his own.

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Sun Dec 22, 2013 6:12 pm Kendrix wrote: Even if there are actual solid differences, Rebuild!Asuka is still meant as a commentary on/reaction to the reaction to the original one as much as anything else in rebuild is a reaction to the reaction of the original and can't be discussed without the context of Original!Asuka..
